Patch Set 4:

Before giving this a plus I have noticed a difference between the linux client and OpenSFS on who uses container_of_safe. Which is correct? I have noticed it but have never sat down to go over the difference yet. Looks like it might be a good time.

Patch Set 4:

Probably OpenSFS uses it where is isn't needed.
That is the case in llite_nfs.c, dt_object.c, lu_object.c
When I converted drivers/staging in 05e6557b8ed8 I dropped and _of0 that didn't need to be.
Certainly when we convert container_of0() in OpenSFS we should check that it really is needed.

parents.subject: LU-13096 llite: serialize max_cached_mb write operation

commit.subject: LU-9859 libcfs: prepare for switch to container_of_safe()
commit.message:
LU-9859 libcfs: prepare for switch to container_of_safe()

Upstream Linux uses container_of_safe() rather than
container_of0().

So provide container_of_safe() for all kernels, and use spelling.txt
to suggest new code uses it.  We can then start switching code
over gradually.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Ie938b76d569de6bb10b51fb0b12d79fcd0f43e7e
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/37736
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
